headlines.title: Energy Prices Drive U.S. Inflation To Three-Year High
headlines.summary: U.S. inflation climbed above 4% for the first time in three years in May as rising energy costs tied to the Iran war pushed consumer costs sharply higher, with the Consumer Price Index rising 0.5% from April and 4.2% from a year earlier, according to data released Wednesday by th

headlines.title: Hormuz Shutdown Forces Malaysia to Overhaul Crude Supply Chain
headlines.summary: Malaysia is looking to diversify its crude sourcing with supply from the U.S., South America, and Africa as the Hormuz crisis has choked off its key Middle Eastern deliveries, Malaysian Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said on Wednesday. headlines.title: UK Economy Set to Shrink as Iran Energy Shock Hits Growth
headlines.summary: The UK economy is expected to contract despite a boost at the start of the year, with economists warning that the war in Iran’s energy price shock will now show up in fresh data.
